name: Anchorage (United States)

colors.symbolism: the field, possibly a reference to the Klondike Gold Rush
the anchor, airplane and lettering of the seal
the central disc bearing the seal

description: A yellow field bearing the seal of the City of Anchorage in the centre — a blue-bordered white circle within which a blue anchor stands in the foreground over a blue airplane, a yellow sun and a yellow sailboat (HMS Resolution); around the disc the words "ANCHORAGE" (above) and "ALASKA" (below) appear in blue.
history: Designed by University of Alaska Anchorage art professor Joan Kimura, who won a 1973 city contest for a first-ever city flag with an acrylic painting that was lightly adjusted before adoption. The city subsequently passed a 1975 resolution to adopt the flag's central design as the city seal.

features.symbolism: yellow field — possibly a reference to the gold of the Klondike Gold Rush, although the city has not formally documented the symbolism

features.role: seal of the City of Anchorage
features.symbolism: the seal — a blue anchor (for the city's name and its origin as an anchorage used during the third voyage of James Cook), a modern airplane (for Anchorage's role as a transportation hub centred on Ted Stevens International Airport), the midnight sun (for the city's extreme variation in daylight at high latitude), and a sailing ship (HMS Resolution under Captain James Cook, who explored Cook Inlet)

features.role: the words "ANCHORAGE" and "ALASKA" encircling the seal
features.symbolism: the city name and state appear in the seal's outer ring
